observations.summary: prepare-tests timed out (rc:124) with salvaged mid-stream output classified as success/COMPLETE — the agent was in its earliest stages (step-start + reasoning only, 2 of 72 trace parts) and produced no ATDD checklist, no test scaffolding, and no story amendments; downstream steps compensated but the 4.11 update_step's sibling-consultation directive was never followed.
observations.evidence: runner-errors.jsonl line 20: {"ts":"2026-07-14T18:22:06Z","source":"opencode-runner","context":"Prepare tests (4.12) / initial run","rc":124,"salvagedOutput":"yes"}. Journal step_end for prepare-tests (status: success, durationMs: 5401949 (~90 min, 4x the 1296480ms average), halts: 0, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt: 'Now let me read the step-02 generation mode file and check the existing test artifacts directory.' — mid-stream, not a completion message). traces/4.12-a1-20260714T163501/prepare-tests.json — default view: 'no narrative in final step' (0 of 72 shown); --narrative (4 of 72 shown) ends at 'Now let me read the step-02 generation mode file and check the existing test artifacts directory.'; --include machinery (2 of 72 shown) shows only [step-start] and [reasoning]. --grep for 'ATDD|checklist|E2E|deferral|scaffold|skip|test.skip' returns 2 matches — both in the prompt text itself, 0 in agent output. Journal step_end for e2e-tests: 'No ATDD checklist exists for story 4.12.' Journal step_end for implement-story lists test files as created by the dev agent (not prepare-tests scaffolding). Journal step_end for review-code: 18 patches applied, several fixing weak test assertions (credential-isolation regex never matched JSON, launch date test asserted any date not the placeholder, missing cron expression assertion, missing section-scoped interval tests). Trends show infra-opencode-timeout has 12 distinct runs; ledger entries record prior occurrences on prepare-tests (4.2, 4.3, 4.9), review-code (3.9, 4.10, 4.11), unit-tests (3.11), test-review (4.4), implement-story (4.5), NFR review (4.4), update-project-context (4.8), create-story (4.9, 4.11).

observations.summary: The 4.11 hypothesized observation is confirmed: 4.11's review-tests never examined auth.credential.spec.ts (0 matches in trace grep), but 4.12 found and fixed a stale transitional marker in that file from Story 1.3 that existed during 4.11's run — the agent's search execution varies in thoroughness despite correct prompt language.
observations.evidence: traces/4.11-a1-20260714T123043/review-tests.json --include machinery --grep 'auth.credential|credential.spec|virtual' returns 0 matches across all 176 parts — the 4.11 agent never mentioned the file. traces/4.12-a1-20260714T163501/review-tests.json --grep 'auth.credential|credential.spec|virtual|stale' (13 of 174 shown): msg_f622b75d7001IHBuDToL9JUYgt part 2 L1: 'Now let me check if crypto.ts exists (to verify the stale transitional marker)'; msg_f622be0b0001Bgc2mzU2bdjqfF part 2 L1: 'Good — crypto.ts exists, confirming the stale transitional marker'; msg_f622f2e12001jJs8Pro5LzO2a0 part 2 L12-18: 'Stale Transitional Marker — FIXED (1 found). apps/web/src/lib/auth.credential.spec.ts:37 (from Story 1.3, found during cross-project search). The comment claimed crypto.ts does not exist until Task 2.1 is implemented alongside a { virtual: true } Jest mock option. But crypto.ts now exists at apps/web/src/lib/crypto.ts.' The marker has existed since Story 1.3 was completed (long before 4.11's run). Ledger entry 4.11 (2026-07-14T16:35:00.735Z, hypothesized): '4.11's search may have been genuinely comprehensive... or it may have missed markers that exist in files the agent did not actually examine — the claim of 114 files searched is not verifiable from the output alone.' Ledger entry 4.10 (2026-07-14T12:30:43.192Z, confirmed): 'review-tests found 35 stale transitional markers across 24 files that 4.6 reported as 0 and 4.8 reported as 3 — all markers existed during those prior runs.' Trends show 4 distinct runs.

observations.summary: 4.12 review-tests found 1 stale marker in auth.credential.spec.ts:37 from Story 1.3 that persisted through 4.10 (which fixed 35 markers) and 4.11 (which reported 0) — the root cause has shifted from implement-story (now working for current-story files) to review-tests' search scope inconsistency.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Stale Transitional Marker — FIXED (1 found). apps/web/src/lib/auth.credential.spec.ts:37 (from Story 1.3, found during cross-project search). The comment claimed crypto.ts does not exist until Task 2.1 is implemented. Fix applied: Removed the stale comment and the unnecessary { virtual: true } option. Verified: all 908 web tests pass.' Journal step_end for implement-story: Story 4.12's own test files (secret-rotation-schedule.spec.ts, check-rotations.spec.ts) are clean — no skips, no stale markers. Ledger entries 146 (4.6, deduced non-recurrence), 154 (4.8, deduced non-recurrence), 4.10 (deduced non-recurrence), 4.11 (deduced non-recurrence) confirm implement-story's update_step is working for current-story files. The marker in auth.credential.spec.ts persisted because review-tests' search missed it in 4.10 and 4.11 (see review-tests-search-scope-misses-component-test-files observation above). Trends show 19 distinct runs.

observations.summary: The 4.11 update_step to e2e-tests is working — the agent autonomously confirmed the E2E deferral without halting (0 halts), even without an ATDD checklist (prepare-tests had timed out), using three independent lines of evidence.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for e2e-tests (halts: 0, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt: 'No ATDD checklist exists for story 4.12... Despite the absence of an ATDD checklist, the deferral is valid and confirmed by three independent lines of evidence: 1. The story's ACs have no browser-level surface. 2. The direct predecessor (4.11) explicitly deferred E2E for identical reasons. 3. The automate-validation-report confirms unit-only coverage. Decision (DP-4): This is a test-only decision... Decided autonomously per DP-4.'). traces/4.12-a1-20260714T163501/e2e-tests.json default view (1 of 76 shown) confirms the agent completed autonomously with no question or escalation. Playbook e2e-tests prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-14T16:35:00.740Z, updatedByRun: 4.11-a1-20260714T123043) includes 'Before generating E2E tests, check the story's ATDD checklist for any E2E deferral decision. If E2E coverage was deferred by prepare-tests because no browser-level mock can simulate the acceptance criteria, confirm the deferral is still valid and complete the step without generating tests or asking for direction.' Ledger entry 4.11 (2026-07-14T16:35:00.734Z, confirmed) records the original observation and applied update_step.

observations.summary: The code review added a concurrency block and timeout-minutes to the workflow YAML but did not add regression guard tests for these additions; the NFR review caught and fixed both gaps.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-code: 'Patches Applied (18)... 3. Added concurrency: block (prevents duplicate issues from overlapping runs).' traces/4.12-a1-20260714T163501/review-code.json --grep 'concurrency|timeout-minutes' (2 of 257 shown): msg_f6224029b00106mXw35F3xGOXB part 2 L1: 'Now patching the workflow YAML — adding concurrency, error handling, and logging.' Journal step_end for review-nfrs: 'Findings (2 found, 2 fixed, 0 deferred) — NFR-1 (MEDIUM): No regression guard test for concurrency block (prevents duplicate issues from overlapping cron + workflow_dispatch runs). FIXED — added [P0] test assertions for concurrency.group and cancel-in-progress. NFR-2 (LOW): No regression guard test for timeout-minutes: 5 (prevents hung workflows). FIXED — added [P0] test assertion for job[timeout-minutes].' Both findings were introduced by the current story's review patches. Playbook review-code prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-13T16:33:16.990Z) says 'After triage, choose apply every patch (no per-finding confirmation) and end — no next-steps menu' — no directive to add tests for new features introduced via patches.
observations.hypothesis: The code review step's prompt directs the agent to apply patches and end, but does not direct it to add regression guard tests for new features or configuration it introduces via patches — this may recur in stories where the code review adds reliability features (concurrency controls, timeouts, error handling) that should have guard tests.
observations.nextStep: Widen into traces/4.12-a1-20260714T163501/review-code.json with --narrative to see whether the agent considered adding tests for the concurrency block and timeout-minutes but decided not to, or whether it never occurred to the agent; if the former, an update_step to review-code directing the agent to add guard tests for new features introduced via patches may be warranted; if the latter, the gap is in the prompt not directing this behavior. Cross-check against prior stories where code review added new features to confirm whether this is a recurring pattern or story-specific.

amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Search all test directories in the project and all test file types (including component test files), not just the story's own test files. Before reporting results, list every test file you examined by path — a claim of comprehensive search is not verifiable without the file list, and prior runs have reported comprehensive coverage while missing files that contained stale markers. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). When the skip reason is no longer applicable — the behavior is covered by other tests, the environmental dependency is permanent with no planned fix, or the test body is empty — remove the skipped test directly rather than only flagging it, since a skipped test that will never be un-skipped is a transitional artifact that inflates the count without verifying behavior. Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation. Treat empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions, only a comment or empty body) as transitional artifacts — remove them directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories in the same directories, since an empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.
amendments.rationale: The 4.11 hypothesized observation is confirmed: review-tests reported 0 stale markers in 4.11 but 4.12 found one in auth.credential.spec.ts that existed during 4.11's run — requiring the agent to list all files examined makes the search verifiable rather than a bare claim, so missed files are detectable from the output alone.
